meta.title: 都市夜行者 - Urban Night Walker (V4)
meta.description: Literary micro CRPG. Engine: Godot 4.7.1, Platform: macOS/Linux. Three layers of expression: shallow (rainy night story), middle (industry intertextuality), deep (self-metaphor). 18 issues.

issues.title: [Research] Theme-Mechanic Mapping
issues.description: Map core themes (despair, hope, identity) to game mechanics (Hope/Despair slider, dialogue branching, environmental changes). Produce mapping chains, harmony scores, and substitution/elimination tests to ensure thematic coherence.
issues.context: Refer to 'Project YOUTH' notes on game-as-expression and identity anxiety. Use '体验引擎' patterns for decision-producing systems. Output a document that maps each thematic beat to a mechanic action.
acceptance_criteria: Document with mapping chains for at least 5 thematic-mechanic pairs.
Harmony scores (1-5) for each pair, with justification.
At least 2 substitution tests and 2 elimination tests documented.

issues.title: [Feature] Project Scaffold
issues.description: Set up Godot 4.7.1 project with CRPG base framework: scene tree, autoloads, singleton GameState, input handling, and a basic test scene.
issues.context: Foundation for all other features. Must adhere to Godot best practices for macOS/Linux. Include placeholder for dialogue engine, UI, and text components.
acceptance_criteria: Project opens without errors on Godot 4.7.1.
Autoload GameState.gd with basic hope/despair variable and signal.
Default scene displays a 3D text label and responds to keyboard input.

issues.title: [Research] Lo-Fi 3D text rendering
issues.description: Investigate Godot 4.7.1 Label3D and shader approaches for rendering text in 3D space with a lo-fi, atmospheric feel (rain, neon, etc.). Prototype simple fragment shaders for color blending and distortion.
issues.context: Inspired by '九十年代素材与文化参考' aesthetic: urban desolation, UFO pulp. Must support dynamic text changes from game state.
acceptance_criteria: Working Label3D with shader that simulates rain streaks and neon glow.
Performance benchmark: 100 text labels at min 30 FPS on macOS/Linux.
Documented shader approach and alternative methods.

issues.title: [Design] Narrative Architecture
issues.description: Design the branching timeline from office to subway station, incorporating 3 endings (Keep Walking, Turn Back, Stay). Define choice points, scene flow, and emotional pacing.
issues.context: Use '体验引擎' world narrative patterns. The narrative should feel environmental, not scripted. Layers: shallow (plot), middle (NPC intertextuality), deep (metaphor for inner projection).
acceptance_criteria: Complete timeline graph with all scenes and choice points leading to 3 endings.
At least 2 narrative echoes (e.g., a line from office reappears in underpass).
Document how the Stranger NPC functions as inner projection and how endings reflect acceptance vs denial.

issues.title: [Feature] Dialogue Engine — data model + conditional branching
issues.description: Implement the data model for dialogue nodes, choices, conditions based on GameState (hope/despair, flags). Use JSON or nested dictionaries for dialogue trees.
issues.context: Must support branching conditions evaluated at runtime. Refer to narrative architecture (issue #4) for structure.
acceptance_criteria: Dialogue node can hold multiple choice branches with condition predicates.
Conditions can read hope/despair slider, flags, and previous choices.
Serialization/deserialization of dialogues from a resource file.

issues.title: [Feature] GameState System
issues.description: Implement the singleton GameState with hope/despair slider (range -10 to +10), flags, and history of choices. Emit signals for state changes to update world text and NPCs.
issues.context: Core feedback system. Must be testable and decoupled. Integrates with dialogue engine and text components.
acceptance_criteria: Slider changes with discrete steps and emits 'state_changed' signal.
Save/load game state for testing.
Flags system works for at least 10 boolean flags.

issues.title: [Feature] Sound System
issues.description: Implement ambient sound: rain (continuous), footsteps (on different surfaces), distant city hum. Use Godot AudioStreamPlayer2D with spatial audio for 3D immersion. Include a low-pass filter for underpass.
issues.context: Enhances literary atmosphere. Sound should respond to slider: more distorted at high despair. Rain volume increases with distance from office?
acceptance_criteria: Rain loop plays continuously, with random variation.
Footstep sounds triggered by dialogue choices (walking).
Underpass scene has reverb/muffled effect.

issues.title: [Feature] Text Component Library
issues.description: Create reusable Label3D scene components for environment text: rain, neon signs, puddles, street lamps. Each component has shader parameters and responds to GameState changes.
issues.context: Lo-fi aesthetic from research (issue #3). Each component must have layered text variants (shallow, middle, deep) based on slider.
acceptance_criteria: 4 base components (rain, neon, puddle, lamppost) with basic visual effects.
Each component has 3 text variants mapped to hope/despair ranges.
Components subtly reflect inner state: e.g., rain text becomes more fragmented at despair.

issues.title: [Design] State-World Feedback
issues.description: Design the Hope/Despair slider and how it affects NPC attitude, environmental text, and available dialogue choices. Define layered responses for each world element.
issues.context: Refer to '体验引擎' decision-producing systems. Each scene must have at least three text variants based on slider state. Use slider as emotional pacing tool.
acceptance_criteria: Slider ranges -10 (despair) to +10 (hope) with 5 discrete states.
Every scene object (rain, neon sign, puddle) has 3 text variants mapped to slider ranges.
Slider influences not just text but also available choices, reflecting internal state change.

issues.title: [Design] Hemingway Writing Constraints
issues.description: Design the style enforcement system: max 25 chars per sentence, max 3 sentences per paragraph. Specify how dialogue and narration will be parsed and enforced at authoring and runtime.
issues.context: Inspired by Hemingway's minimalism. This constraint forces poetic economy. The system should flag violations during content creation and enforce at display time.
acceptance_criteria: Documented constraint rules and examples.
Design for an editor-side validation script (future).
Runtime enforcement: text longer than limit is truncated or triggers UI warning.

issues.title: [Feature] Dialogue Engine — runtime + visual presentation
issues.description: Implement the runtime system that processes dialogue nodes, displays text in 3D space (using Label3D), handles player input to advance, and applies Hemingway constraints.
issues.context: Must work with Text Component Library (issue #8). Use signals to communicate with GameState and UISystem.
acceptance_criteria: Player can navigate a dialogue tree in 3D space via keyboard.
Hemingway constraint truncates text to max 25 chars / 3 sentences.
Dialogue choices trigger GameState updates (hope/despair change).

issues.title: [Feature] UI System
issues.description: Implement minimal Hopper-style UI: text display area, choice list, and status bar showing hope/despair. All in 3D space using Label3D or CanvasLayer as needed.
issues.context: Minimalist, non-intrusive. Should not break immersion. Use Godot's UI containers but keep text in 3D.
acceptance_criteria: Choice list appears as 3D floating labels above dialogue text.
Hope/despair indicator shown as a thin bar at bottom of screen.
No overlapping or clipped text; responsive to different aspect ratios.

issues.title: [Feature] NPC Framework + Convenience Clerk
issues.description: Create generic NPC framework (dialogue tree, state, sprite label) and implement the Convenience Clerk as first NPC. Clerk has layered personality: tired worker, cynical veteran, systemic exhaustion.
issues.context: From design notes: NPC intertextuality. Clerk should comment on player's state. Three layers correspond to different slider ranges. Use dialogue engine (issues #5, #11).
acceptance_criteria: Clerk has 3 dialogue branches (greeting, purchase, farewell).
When hope > 5, clerk is more cheerful; when despair < -5, clerk becomes nihilistic.
Clerk's lines contain subtle references to player's office choice, revealing a shared resignation.

issues.title: [Scene] Office Door → Street → Convenience Store
issues.description: Build the first playable scene sequence: player leaves office (doorway), walks along rainy street, enters convenience store. Include branching dialogue with clerk and environmental text components.
issues.context: This is the opening of the game. Must set atmosphere and introduce Hope/Despair slider through dialogue choices. Layered: shallow (moving forward), middle (clerk's intertextuality), deep (office as symbol of routine despair).
acceptance_criteria: Player can move from office to convenience store through dialogue choices, with proper scene transitions.
Convenience store clerk (NPC) has 3 dialogue branches that reference earlier choices (e.g., 'You look tired').
Environmental texts (office window, street sign, neon) reflect hope/despair and foreshadow the Stranger.

issues.title: [Feature] Story Content — script for all scenes + 3 endings
issues.description: Write the full script: all dialogue lines, environmental texts, and ending sequences. Ensure Hemingway constraints are met. Integrate into dialogue data files.
issues.context: Content creation. Must align with narrative architecture and layered expressions. Each line must have at least shallow and middle layers; deep layer optional but encouraged for key moments.
acceptance_criteria: All scenes have complete dialogue and environmental text (100% coverage).
At least 5 instances of intertextuality across scenes (e.g., phrase repeated in different context).
The three endings each carry a distinct emotional arc: Keep Walking (faith), Turn Back (give up), Stay (acceptance).

issues.title: [Testing] MVP Playtest & Layered Verification
issues.description: Conduct playtest of full MVP vertical slice (issues 1-17). Verify each acceptance criteria layer (shallow, middle, deep). Document bugs, pacing issues, and thematic coherence.
issues.context: Must have at least 3 testers. Use a checklist derived from acceptance criteria. Note any broken dependencies or missing features.
acceptance_criteria: All shallow acceptance criteria pass for each scene and feature.
At least 60% of middle criteria pass; failing ones documented with suggestions.
Deep layer evaluated qualitatively: at least 2 testers report feeling the metaphor/introspection.

issues.title: [Scene] Convenience Store → Bridge → Underpass
issues.description: Build the second scene sequence: player exits store, crosses a bridge (over a dark canal), descends into an underpass. Culminates in encounter with Mysterious Stranger. Branching dialogue options lead to three endings.
issues.context: Emotional climax. Bridge scene should echo 'death-and-rebirth' themes from Project YOUTH. Underpass as liminal space. Layered: shallow (walking), middle (bridge text recalls office), deep (Stranger's true nature revealed only on deep playthrough).
acceptance_criteria: Player navigates from store to underpass with 3 scene transitions.
Bridge text changes based on earlier convenience store choice; Stranger's dialogue has two visible layers.
Underpass has a hidden text (only visible at despair < -5) that reveals the Stranger as projection.

issues.title: [Feature] Mysterious Stranger NPC
issues.description: Implement the Mysterious Stranger NPC with core dialogue tree that has 3 truth layers. The Stranger is an ambiguous figure: inner projection, doppelganger, or supernatural? Choices determine ending.
issues.context: Key to narrative depth. Refer to narrative architecture (issue #4). The Stranger's dialogue should shift based on hope/despair and previous choices. Layered: shallow (mysterious helper/adversary), middle (mirror of player), deep (symbol of acceptance/rejection).
acceptance_criteria: Stranger appears in underpass with 3 distinct dialogue paths leading to 3 endings.
Player's previous choices (office, store, bridge) influence Stranger's lines and attitude.
On second playthrough (or specific flag), Stranger's dialogue reveals 'I am you' – the game-as-metaphor layer.

mvp.name: Minimal Viable Product
mvp.description: Design first (theme-mechanic + narrative architecture + Hemingway constraints) + core engine (dialogue, GameState, UI, text components) + complete 2-scene path with Stranger NPC and 3 endings.

v1.name: Content Complete
v1.description: Full script content + sound system + MVP playtest and layered verification.

full.name: Full Release
full.description: All 18 issues complete: full game with all scenes, NPCs, endings, sound, and verified layered experience.
